data.title: Buff Spleen
data.shortDescription: Buff Spleen
data.fullDescription:
Buff spleen, better known as beef spleen, is a nutrient-rich organ meat gaining popularity among both health-conscious humans and pet owners seeking natural nutrition. For humans, beef spleen is a powerhouse of heme iron, vitamin B12, zinc, and selenium—nutrients essential for energy production, immune support, and red blood cell formation. Traditional cultures have long valued organ meats like spleen for their ability to nourish and strengthen the blood, especially during recovery from illness or fatigue. Thanks to its high bioavailability, even small amounts of beef spleen can deliver a dense dose of vital micronutrients, often consumed as cooked food, dehydrated powder, or encapsulated supplements.

In the pet world—especially for dogs and cats—buff spleen is considered a valuable addition to a raw or species-appropriate diet. It provides natural iron and enzymes that support healthy blood function and immunity. Because it mimics the ancestral diet of wild carnivores, beef spleen is especially popular among raw feeders and holistic veterinarians. Freeze-dried or raw spleen is commonly used as a topper, treat, or supplement to boost vitality and address deficiencies naturally. Whether it’s fueling a working dog or supporting a tired human immune system, buff spleen proves that sometimes the most overlooked foods are the most powerful.

data.title: Buff Marrow / Soup Bones
data.shortDescription: Buff Marrow Bones – Rich, Nutritious & Flavorful
data.fullDescription: Buff marrow bones (from buffalo) are a highly nutritious and flavorful source of natural goodness for both humans and pets. Packed with collagen, healthy fats, and minerals like calcium, phosphorus, and magnesium, marrow bones support joint health, strong bones, and improved immunity. For pets, especially dogs, buff marrow bones make an excellent natural chew that helps clean teeth, strengthen jaws, and reduce anxiety through healthy gnawing. When used in cooking, slow-roasted or simmered buffalo marrow adds deep umami flavor and richness to soups and broths while providing essential nutrients for skin, hair, and gut health.In short, buff marrow bones are a wholesome, protein-rich superfood—perfect for nourishment, flavor, and overall well-being.

data.title: Emu Boneless Fatless
data.shortDescription: Emu Boneless & Fatless Meat – Lean, Healthy, and Protein-Rich
data.fullDescription:
Emu boneless and fatless meat is a premium lean red meat known for its exceptional nutrition and taste. It contains high-quality protein with all essential amino acids while being low in fat, cholesterol, and calories, making it an ideal choice for health-conscious individuals.

Rich in iron, zinc, and vitamin B12, emu meat supports energy production, muscle growth, and heart health. Its soft texture and mild flavor make it versatile for grilling, roasting, or stir-frying.

In short, emu boneless and fatless meat is a heart-friendly, nutrient-dense alternative to traditional red meats—perfect for those seeking fitness, wellness, and clean nutrition.

data.title: Chicken Wings
data.shortDescription: Chicken Wings – Tasty, Protein-Packed & Versatile
data.fullDescription:
Chicken wings are one of the most flavorful and satisfying cuts of chicken, loved for their crispy texture and juicy taste. They are a good source of high-quality protein, which helps in muscle building and repair, and contain essential nutrients like vitamin B6, niacin, phosphorus, and selenium that support metabolism and immune function.

When prepared healthily—grilled, baked, or air-fried—chicken wings can be a nutritious, energy-boosting snack or meal. Their natural collagen content also contributes to joint and skin health.

In short, chicken wings offer the perfect balance of taste, nutrition, and enjoyment for any occasion.

data.shortDescription: The word "buffalo" comes from the French word bœuf, which was used by French fur trappers in the early 1600s to describe bison. Bœuf was also the name the French used for the true buffalo, which live in Africa and Asia.

data.title: Buff Tenderloin with Fat
data.shortDescription: You can distinguish between adult males and females by looking at their horns. If the base of their two horns come together and form a solid structure (called a boss) then it is a male. The horns of an adult buffalo cow don't touch. When male Cape buffaloes fight they use their horns and their massive muscular bodies to administer heavy blows to their opponents.
data.fullDescription: It's a rich source of high-quality protein, essential vitamins such as B6, B12, thiamine, niacin, and minerals like selenium and zinc. The Meat Story Buff Tenderloin is Small, thin, lean, incredibly tender muscles located on either side of the backbone, tenderloins are the most prized cut on the entire animal. Their buttery tenderness is legendary. It is considered as lean meat for dogs

data.fullDescription: Chicken with Skin and Bone is the full-cast director’s cut of chicken, bringing the juicy drama, crispy potential, and rich flavor that boneless cuts only daydream about. It is rustic, bold, and unapologetically delicious, like the chicken equivalent of arriving late to a party and still stealing the spotlight.

data.title: Chicken Skinless with Bone
data.shortDescription: Hens communicate with their chicks while they’re still inside their eggs! How adorable is that? When chickens lay eggs, the mother hen can actually make noises that chicks still incubating inside of their eggs can hear and respond to. The chicks even make tiny “peeps” back from inside of their eggs! Once grown, chickens utilize a wide range of sounds to communicate with each other.
data.fullDescription: Boiled chicken is the best option by shredding and discarding the bones, whether your pup is sick or you're just looking for a healthy food to feed your dog. In fact, boiled chicken is filled with protein, vitamins, and minerals that your dog's diet requires and is gentle enough to feed to dogs with sensitive or upset stomachs.

data.shortDescription: Chickens have a great memory for faces! It’s true! Chickens have the ability to recognize and remember around 100 different faces (human, and chicken!). If you have backyard chickens and they get excited when they see you or their favorite people, that’s why!
data.fullDescription: Cooked chicken is a healthy food that is safe for most dogs to eat. data.title: Chicken Leg Boneless
data.shortDescription: Chickens are actually omnivores, not herbivores. While some people think that chickens eat only plants and grains, they actually eat (and enjoy) a much wider variety of foods! Chickens do indeed eat plants, seeds, and grains, but also bits of dairy or meat products. Kitchen scraps can be used for a supplement to their feed, which makes for an environmentally-friendly way to handle leftover food waste. Chickens also like to peck around in the dirt and find bugs to eat, for example, beetles, larvae, slugs, and grasshoppers.
data.fullDescription: we should use plain, boneless, and skinless chicken breasts or chicken thighs. Chicken bones, uncooked or cooked, should always be avoided as they can cause a gastrointestinal blockage or perforation.

data.title: Chicken Fatless Minced
data.shortDescription: Some research suggests that chickens are just as clever as human toddlers. Hens have exhibited mathematical reasoning, object permanence, self-control, and even structural engineering. Chickens also demonstrate empathy and a number of emotional responses!
data.fullDescription: The Meat Story Chicken mince is a lean and protein-packed choice that supports lean muscle mass and overall health. The rich content of iron and zinc ensure that your dog is getting essential nutrients for growth and repair, while the omega 6 fatty acids promote healthy skin and a shiny coat.

data.title: Mutton Neck
data.shortDescription: Lamb necks are excellent source of protein, calcium and phosphorous for dogs. These are prefect for big chewers, larger dogs and probably the largest bones we recommend feeding.

data.title: Mutton Spine
data.shortDescription: A halved mutton spine with some small ribs attached - ideal for teething puppies (especially when served frozen) or as a treat for dogs of all sizes. Suitable for puppies and adult dogs. All dogs should be supervised when feeding a bone.
